methods.description: Process for extracting macro-charcoal particles from lake sediment cores for subsequent analysis. This involves physical slicing, subsampling, chemical treatment to differentiate charcoal from organic matter, sieving to isolate macro-charcoal, and measuring particle surface area.

steps.description: cut the basal sediments continuously into 0.5 -cm-thick slices.
steps.searchable_summary: Cut sediment core into 0.5 cm thick slices

steps.description: extracted a 1-cm 3 subsample from the middle of each slice to avoid contamination risk (Mustaphi and Pisaric, 2014).
steps.searchable_summary: Extract 1 cm³ subsample from the middle of each sediment slice

steps.description: Bathed the subsample in a solution of potassium hydroxide (KOH), sodium hexametaphosphate (Na 6 P 6 O 18 ), and sodium hypochlorite (NaClO) for at least 24 h under agitation to perform deflocculation and to help differentiate charcoal particles from organic matter (Whitlock and Larsen, 2001).
steps.searchable_summary: Chemically treat sediment subsample with KOH, Na6P6O18, and NaClO solution under agitation for deflocculation and differentiation

steps.description: sieved the solution through a 160 µm mesh to extract macro-charcoal particles
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve treated sediment suspension through a 160 µm mesh to isolate macro-charcoal particles

steps.description: measured their surface area under a dissecting microscope using the WinSEEDLE ™ image analysis software (Regents Instruments Inc., https://regent.qc.ca/, August 2021).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure surface area of macro-charcoal particles using image analysis software

methods.method_name: Charcoal Size Distribution (CSD) Analysis for Local Fire History
methods.description: Application of the Charcoal Size Distribution (CSD) method to reconstruct local fire history from charcoal particle size data. This involves calculating geometric mean diameters, sorting particles into size classes, performing linear regression on size distribution, and classifying fire events based on regression slope and particle size thresholds.

steps.description: applied the Charcoal Size Distribution (CSD) method (Asselin and Payette, 2005;Oris et al., 2014a;Remy et al., 2018) to every fire event detected using the CharAnalysis software v1.1 (Higuera et al., 2008; http://CharAnalysis.googlepages.com).
steps.searchable_summary: Apply Charcoal Size Distribution (CSD) method to fire events detected by CharAnalysis software
inputs: Charcoal particle data (surface area or diameter)

steps.description: calculated geometric mean diameters of charcoal particles from the square root of the surface area (Clark and Hussey, 1996)
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate geometric mean diameter from the square root of charcoal particle surface area
Convert charcoal particle geometric mean diameters to log mm scale
outputs: Charcoal particle diameters in log mm

steps.description: Sorted charcoal particles of each sample according to their size into six classes ranging from −0.9 log mm to 0.3 log mm (every 0.2 log mm).
steps.searchable_summary: Sort charcoal particles into predefined size classes based on log mm diameter
inputs: Charcoal particle diameters in log mm
Predefined size classes (-0.9 to 0.3 log mm, 0.2 log mm intervals)
outputs: Count or proportion of particles per size class for each sample

steps.description: Applied a linear regression to the distribution of the proportion of charcoal particles in each size class (−0.8, −0.6, etc.) for each sample.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform linear regression on the proportion of charcoal particles in each size class versus the size class value
inputs: Proportion of charcoal particles per size class for each sample
outputs: Linear regression slope for each sample's size distribution

steps.description: classified as local fires (on the paleo-island) the samples with a slope more gentle than a threshold fixed at −1.77 based on a previous study (Oris et al., 2014a).
steps.searchable_summary: Classify fire events as local if the linear regression slope of the charcoal size distribution is gentler (greater) than -1.77
inputs: Linear regression slope for each sample

steps.description: considered as local fires only the samples having at least one charcoal particle >−0.3 log mm (Supplemental Figure 1).
steps.searchable_summary: Refine local fire classification by requiring at least one charcoal particle larger than -0.3 log mm in the sample
inputs: Charcoal particle diameters in log mm for each sample
outputs: Final classification of local fire events

steps.description: considered that other fires (extra-local or regional) did not burn on the watersheds of the paleo-islands.
steps.searchable_summary: Classify all fire events not meeting the local criteria as extra-local or regional
inputs: Final classification of local fire events
outputs: Final classification of all fire events (local/extra-local/regional)

methods.method_name: Site Selection and Lake Sediment Sampling
methods.description: Methodology for selecting study sites (lakes) and collecting lake sediment cores for paleoenvironmental analysis. This involves selecting lakes based on specific criteria and using a Livingstone corer to extract complete sediment sequences.

steps.description: selected two small headwater lakes surrounded by steep shores to maximize sediment collection: Lake Perché (formerly known as Lake Edrien) in the Aiguebelle National Park (Abijévis hills) and Lake Despériers on the Kekeko hills (Table 1; Figure 2).
steps.searchable_summary: Select study lakes based on criteria (small, headwater, steep shores, location)
inputs: Site selection criteria (small, headwater, steep shores)
outputs: Selected lake locations (Lake Perché, Lake Despériers)

steps.description: extracted lake sediment cores from the center of each lake with a 60 mm diameter Livingstone corer.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract lake sediment cores from the center of selected lakes using a Livingstone corer

steps.description: Collected complete gyttja sequences (organic sediments), from the water-sediment interface to the contact with the underlying mineral glacial sediments.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect complete organic sediment (gyttja) sequences within the cores
Store sediment cores in plastic tubes

steps.description: kept them in cold conditions (4°C) until further analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: Store sediment tubes in cold conditions (4°C)
